title: fix: cancel timed-out Replicate predictions
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Replicate predictions could continue running—and consuming provider resources—after our local polling timeout returned a 504.

- Sends `Cancel-After: 6m` when creating a prediction, covering the 60-second synchronous wait and five-minute polling budget.
- Cancels a still-running prediction when the local polling budget is exhausted, using Replicate’s supplied cancellation URL or the standard fallback endpoint.
- Bounds best-effort cancellation to five seconds and preserves the original 504 if cancellation fails or never settles; the create-time deadline remains the cleanup safety net.
- Covers the deadline header, custom and fallback cancellation URLs, cancellation failures, and a never-settling cancellation request.

Tests:
- `npx vitest run test/image/replicateClient.test.ts`
- `npx vitest run test/image`
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npx biome check src/image/utils/replicateClient.ts test/image/replicateClient.test.ts`

gist.headline: No prediction left buzzing
gist.blurb: Timed-out Replicate jobs now get a polite but firm recall notice. Polli has started turning off the garden hose before wandering back inside.
gist.summary: Replicate image predictions now receive a `Cancel-After: 6m` deadline at creation and are actively cancelled after Pollinations' five-minute polling budget expires, with a five-second best-effort cleanup window.
gist.impact: When an image request reaches its 504 timeout, its still-running Replicate prediction is cleaned up through Replicate's supplied cancellation URL or the standard fallback endpoint, helping keep provider work tidy without delaying the original response.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Polli as a careful garden keeper shutting off a runaway pixel-art watering hose connected to a glowing Replicate-style image-prediction machine. A small retro timer shows six minutes while a five-minute cleanup clock and a neat cancellation lever convey bounded, reliable timeout cleanup; include code-plants and a cozy garden-server setting.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow bee mascot with dark brown stripes, black oval eyes, bright pink rectangular cheek blushes, a happy smile, light blue translucent wings, and a tiny gardener tool belt, carefully turning off a glowing lime green (#ecf874) watering hose connected to a retro image-prediction terminal as a small pixel timer reads "6M"; code-plants, flowers, and vines grow around the tidy garden server room. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, with dark purple (#110518) outlines and subtle CRT glow.
